children.heading: Funding priorities
children.label: a Funding priorities
children.text: The Secretary shall prioritize centers of excellence established for purposes of carrying out research, extension, and education activities relating to the food and agricultural sciences (as defined in ) for the receipt of funding for any competitive research or extension program administered by the Secretary.section 3103 of this title

children.heading: Composition
children.label: b Composition
children.text: A center of excellence is composed of 1 or more of the eligible entities specified in subsection (b)(7) of that provide financial or in-kind support to the center of excellence.section 3157 of this title

children.heading: Criteria for centers of excellence
children.label: c Criteria for centers of excellence
A
children.text: to ensure coordination and cost effectiveness by reducing unnecessarily duplicative efforts regarding research, teaching, and extension;

children.label: B
children.text: to leverage available resources by using public-private partnerships among agricultural industry groups, institutions of higher education, and the Federal Government;

children.label: C
children.text: to implement teaching initiatives to increase awareness and effectively disseminate solutions to target audiences through extension activities; and

children.label: D
children.text: to increase the economic returns to rural communities by identifying, attracting, and directing funds to high-priority agricultural issues.

children.heading: Additional efforts
children.label: 2 Additional efforts
children.text: Where practicable, the criteria for recognition as a center of excellence shall include efforts to improve teaching capacity and infrastructure at colleges and universities (including land-grant colleges and universities, cooperating forestry schools, NLGCA Institutions (as those terms are defined in ), and schools of veterinary medicine).section 3103 of this title

children.heading: Centers of excellence at 1890s Institutions
children.label: d Centers of excellence at 1890s Institutions

children.heading: Recognition
children.label: 1 Recognition
children.text: The Secretary shall recognize not less than 3 centers of excellence, each led by an 1890 Institution (as defined in ), to focus on 1 or more of the areas described in paragraph (2).section 7601 of this title

children.heading: Student success and workforce development
children.label: A Student success and workforce development
children.text: A center of excellence established under paragraph (1) may engage in activities to ensure that students have the skills and education needed to work in agriculture and food industries, agriculture science, technology, engineering, mathematics, and related fields of study.

children.heading: Nutrition, health, wellness, and quality of life
children.label: B Nutrition, health, wellness, and quality of life
children.text: A center of excellence established under paragraph (1) may carry out research, education, and extension programs that increase access to healthy food, improve nutrition, mitigate preventive disease, and develop strategies to assist limited resource individuals in accessing health and nutrition resources.

children.heading: Farming systems, rural prosperity, and economic sustainability
children.label: C Farming systems, rural prosperity, and economic sustainability
children.text: A center of excellence established under paragraph (1) may share best practices with farmers to improve agricultural production, processing, and marketing, reduce urban food deserts, examine new uses for traditional and nontraditional crops, animals, and natural resources, and continue activities carried out by the Center for Innovative and Sustainable Small Farms, Ranches, and Forest Lands.

children.heading: Global food security and defense
children.label: D Global food security and defense
children.text: A center of excellence established under paragraph (1) may engage in international partnerships that strengthen agricultural development in developing countries, partner with international researchers regarding new and emerging animal and plant pests and diseases, engage in agricultural disaster recovery, and continue activities carried out by the Center for International Engagement.

children.heading: Natural resources, energy, and environment
children.label: E Natural resources, energy, and environment
children.text: A center of excellence established under paragraph (1) may focus on protecting and managing domestic natural resources for current and future production of food and agricultural products.

children.heading: Emerging technologies
children.label: F Emerging technologies
children.text: A center of excellence established under paragraph (1) may focus on the development of emerging technologies to increase agricultural productivity, enhance small farm economic viability, and improve rural communities by developing genetic and sensor technologies for food and agriculture and providing technology training to farmers.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 3 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection $10,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023.

children.label: A
children.text: the resources invested in the centers of excellence established under paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: the work being done by those centers of excellence.
